history.content:
<thought>
I am located at the bottom center, facing forward. The cell directly ahead is a wall (#), so I cannot move forward. The right side appears to have free floor cells, indicating a possible path to navigate around the wall. I should turn right to explore this path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at cell D07, which is a '.' (floor and safe). The cells moving forward look clear for now. To reach the goal, I should continue moving forward to explore more while avoiding lava cells visible on the left side. So the next action should be to move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at row 7 column D. The cell in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is impossible. To find a safe path, I will turn left to explore the space on my left side where there seem to be floor cells ('.') with no immediate danger of lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see that I am located at row 7, column D (marked with ^). The only open floor cells nearby are mostly to the left, right, and straight ahead. The corridor to the right (columns E and F) is clear, while ahead and left are blocked partially or by lava. Since I want to avoid lava cells, moving forward or left could be dangerous as there is lava further ahead. The right side seems safer and open to explore further.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward at cell D7, which is clear ('.'). The cells directly ahead (D6) is also a floor ('.') and safe to move into. Lava cells 'L' are to my left and forward-left, so I should avoid those directions. The path ahead seems safe, so I will move forward to continue exploring and find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I observe that I am positioned at the bottom-center of the grid facing forward at cell D7. The path ahead appears blocked by a wall at cell E7. There is lava on cell C7 and L7, so moving left or backward is unsafe. I see an open path on cell C6 and C5, suggesting the best route is to turn left to avoid the lava and wall, then move forward along the open floor to navigate towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am located at row 7, column D, facing upward. The immediate cell in front of me (row 6, column D) is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. To avoid lava, I should turn right to face East, where the path seems clear and safe.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward at position D7. The cell straight ahead (D6) is empty floor (.), safe to move into. The goal is not visible in this local view, so the strategy is to move forward onto safe ground and then explore more. Moving forward is safe and the best immediate action.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward from position D7. The cell directly in front (D6) is a floor cell, which is safe to move into. The goal is not yet visible in the local observation, but moving forward on the safe floor cell will help me progress further into unexplored areas. Therefore, I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward at row 7 column D. Ahead is a wall (#) at column E, so moving forward is blocked. To proceed safely and explore more of the map, I will turn left to face upward and possibly find a path around the obstacles and lava cells.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at cell D7 which is empty. The goal G is visible at F1, which is quite far and up ahead. Direct forward movement is blocked by lava in several rows ahead. Moving forward here is safe, so I will advance forward to get closer and assess the path further.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at position D07. The cell in front of me at D06 is lava (L), so moving forward is dangerous. To reach the goal at F02, I need to find a safe path up and to the right. Turning left will face me towards C07, which is also lava, so that's unsafe. Turning right will face me toward E07, which is lava. So turning left or right would face me towards lava, moving forward is lava too. I should try turning around by turning right twice. Let's start by turning right once to reassess with a new direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I see that I am facing forward at position D7. The cell directly ahead at D6 is lava, which is dangerous to move into. To avoid danger, I should choose to turn left or right to find a safer path around the lava cells blocking the way forward. Turning left will face me towards C, which is an empty floor cell, so turning left is safer.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am at the bottom center facing forward. The cell right in front of me is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. Looking to the sides, I have a free path on my left (cell D6 is floor), while the right side is blocked with lava. To find a safe route toward the goal visible at row 2, column F, I will turn left to face the free path to the left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
My current position is at row 07, column D, facing forward towards row 06, column D which is a lava cell. I cannot move forward onto lava. To find a safe path, I should turn right to face column E, where the cell is empty floor, providing a safe direction. 
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward at cell D07. Forward is a lava cell, which is deadly, so moving forward is not an option. To reach the goal at F02, the agent needs to find a safe path. Turning left will face the agent toward C07, which is a lava cell as well. Turning right faces the agent toward E07, which is lava too. The only safe option is to turn around by turning right twice to face backward, towards D06, which is a floor cell. This will allow the agent to move forward safely in the next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward on the cell D7. The forward cell D6 is lava, so moving forward is not safe. To avoid the lava and continue exploring, I should turn either left or right. Looking at the observation, the right side (E7) is free space ('.'). I will turn right to move toward that safe cell.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward into row 6, column D. The cell in front is a floor cell, safe to move into. The goal is not visible yet, but moving forward will keep exploring the map while avoiding lava, which are to the sides and behind. Therefore, moving forward is the safest next action.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am currently at row 07 column D facing forward as indicated by '^'. The row ahead (row 06) is all walls '#', so I cannot move forward. I need to turn to find an accessible path. I will turn left to face towards an open floor cell on my left side.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is located at row 7 column D facing up. The cell directly ahead (row 6, column D) is a floor (safe to move), no lava or wall. Moving forward will safely move closer to exploring the area. Therefore, I will move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see that directly in front of me is a wall (#), so moving forward is not possible. To navigate around the wall, I should turn left or right. On the left side, based on the map, there is a floor cell (.). Turning left will face me toward open floor cells, which is safer than turning right where I may encounter unknown or blocked cells. I will turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I see the goal at row 01, column E, which is roughly diagonally forward and to the right from my current position at row 07, column D. However, I can't move forward immediately because the space directly in front (row 06, column D) is lava. I need to avoid lava, so moving forward is not safe. Turning to the right looks promising because column E is clear and close to the goal, and the cell to the right side (row 07, column E) is free ('.'). I will turn right to face that direction, then I can move forward towards the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward towards column D in row 6, which is a floor cell (safe to move). There are lava cells to the left of the agent, so I must avoid moving in that direction. Since the cell ahead is safe and not lava or wall, I'll move forward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward at cell D07. The cell just in front of me is a wall (#) at D06, so I cannot move forward. To navigate safely around the lava at C07, I will turn right to face towards the free floor cell at E07.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cells in front of me are floor cells, safe to move onto. The row above has many unseen cells which might contain the goal or other obstacles. I will move forward to explore further, as moving forward is the safest immediate action to advance towards potential goal cells.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
